text.title: ops-financial-planning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ops-financial-planning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Conflating owner draw with operating profit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixing them obscures true business health. Owner pay is a cost of the business. If the business cannot afford to pay the owner a market wage, it is not yet profitable.

failure-mode.description: Building a 12-month forecast from desired revenue rather than from known customer count and ARPU.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Top-down forecasts hide the actual growth gap. Bottom-up forecasts reveal exactly how many customers at what price are needed to hit the target.

failure-mode.description: Applying the reinvestment framework to an exceptional month with one-time revenue.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One-time payments skew the allocation. Non-recurring revenue must be flagged separately before applying tier thresholds; only recurring revenue determines the tier.
